stories.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
stories.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

stories.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
stories.description: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran talks are uncertain as ceasefire is set to expire
stories.description: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.

stories.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
stories.description: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
stories.description: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

stories.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.description: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran vows retaliation soon for what it calls an "act of armed piracy", which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Lebanon’s Christians support displaced amid ceasefire uncertainty
stories.description: As Lebanese authorities work to extend the ceasefire, many people on the ground continue to live in deep uncertainty. The country’s Christian community is supporting those displaced by the conflict while also coping with its own grief and disappointment. Serge Berberi and Renee D...

stories.title: Middle East: Doubts grow over US-Iran talks
stories.description: The Iranian delegation has not yet departed for scheduled peace talks with the US in Islamabad, Iranian state TV said on Tuesday. US Vice President JD Vance is reportedly en route for Pakistan but Iran has not yet confirmed that it will attend the negotiations. France24 Internati...

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon to hold news talks
stories.description: The United States is set to host new talks on Thursday between Israel and Lebanon in an effort to reach an agreement as their fragile ceasefire largely holds. The discussions will take place at the State Department in Washington, once again at the ambassadorial level. Lebanese Pr...

stories.title: Pakistan prepares for potential talks
stories.description: The Iranian delegation has not yet departed for scheduled peace talks with the US in Islamabad, Iranian state TV said on Tuesday. US Vice President JD Vance is reportedly en route for Pakistan but Iran has not yet confirmed that it will attend the negotiations. France24 correspon...

stories.title: Doubt surrounds potential US-Iran talks
stories.description: Time is running out as the ceasefire between Iran and the US is set to expire tomorrow, Wednesday. Despite uncertainty over who will actually attend, preparations continue for a new round of peace talks in Islamabad. Major issues, such as control of the Strait of Hormuz, remain u...

stories.title: Lebanon negotiating with Israel to obtain 'serenity, prosperity': Ghassan Salamé
stories.description: François Picard welcomes Ghassan Salamé, Lebanon's culture minister. Salamé portrays Lebanon as a country that has preserved political pluralism and freedom of expression, and persevered despite decades of conflict. Within this context, he frames negotiation with Israel not as a ...

stories.title: Macron to meet Lebanon's PM in Paris to 'reaffirm commitment' for Israel truce
stories.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam is set to meet with French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace late on Tuesday, with the president expected to reaffirm his support for the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, three days after the death of a French peacekeeper in...

stories.title: Ghassan Salamé: Lebanon negotiating with Israel 'to give serenity, prosperity to fellow countrymen'
stories.description: François Picard welcomes Ghassan Salamé, Lebanon’s Minister of Culture. Salamé portrays Lebanon as a country that has preserved political pluralism and freedom of expression, and persevered despite decades of conflict. Within this context, he frames negotiation with Israel not as...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: How a threat became a playbook
stories.description: How the Strait of Hormuz became a system of control, shaping power and the global economy.

stories.title: Is China a winner of the Iran war or facing economic risks?
stories.description: China's economy beats expectations despite the Iran war, but weak demand and structural risks remain.

stories.title: As Iran crisis drags on, fears of global food catastrophe grow
stories.description: While global food prices have risen modestly so far, the full impact of the conflict has yet to be felt, analysts say.

stories.title: My dreams in Iran were already dead before the ceasefire came
stories.description: The bombs have stopped falling for now. But the damage is done, and my life has been wrecked.

stories.title: US, Iran exchange threats as fragile ceasefire set to expire
stories.description: The US and Iran trade threats as tensions escalate between the countries before their two-week ceasefire ends.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 53 of the US-Israel conflict?
stories.description: Diplomatic efforts to end the US-Israel war on Iran are stalled as Tehran rejects talks under pressure.

stories.title: Iran war forces job losses, reverse migration in India’s ceramic hub
stories.description: The ceramics industry in Gujarat's Morbi has been badly hit by fuel crisis, with most units closed and workers laid off.

stories.title: US veterans arrested in Capitol during protest against the war on Iran
stories.description: US veterans were arrested while staging a protest in the US Capitol building in opposition to the war on Iran.

stories.title: Diplomatic flurry as US-Iran truce deadline nears
stories.description: • US team heading to Islamabad today; Iran also ‘considering’ sending negotiators• Trump extends ceasefire by a day, says further extension ‘highly unlikely’• Pezeshkian criticises ‘bitter, contradictory’ messages from US; Iran speaker says engaged in talks but also ready for ‘ne...

stories.title: ‘Biggest-ever’ US-Philippines war games begin
stories.description: MANILA: Thousands of American and Philippine troops, joined for the first time by a significant contingent of Japanese forces, began annual military exercises on Monday set against the backdrop of the Middle East war. The war games will feature live-fire exercises in the north of...

stories.title: Pope decries exploitation by world’s ‘authoritarians’
stories.description: SAURIMO: Pope Leo lamented during an event in Angola on Monday that many people in the world were being “exploited by authoritarians and defrauded by the rich”, the latest example of a forceful new speaking style he has adopted on his four-nation Africa tour. The first US pope, w...

stories.title: Hungary will carry out ICC warrant against Netanyahu, says Magyar
stories.description: BUDAPEST: Hungary’s incoming prime minister Peter Magyar said on Monday the country will execute International Criminal Court warrants against anyone, after he invited Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu — wanted under just such a warrant — to Budapest later this year. In 2024, the...

stories.title: Amnesty highlights erosion of civil liberties in Pakistan
stories.description: • Says cybercrime, anti-terror laws are weaponised to silence voices; authorities pull ads from critical newspapers• Warns world faces unprecedented rights crisis; cites use of AI, tech to expand surveillance, curb online dissent LONDON: Global rights watchdog Amnesty Inter­natio...

stories.title: India, South Korea plan $50bn trade push with new deals
stories.description: India and South Korea said on Monday that they would boost their economic ties by expanding cooperation in energy, critical minerals, shipbuilding, semiconductors and steel as they seek to double their trade to $50 billion by 2030. New Delhi and Seoul also agreed to resume and st...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: No Iranian delegation has yet departed for talks with U.S. in Pakistan, says state media
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards have warned of targeting any vessel attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz without permission.

stories.title: No delegation from Iran visits Islamabad, state television says, as talks speculation grows
stories.description: So far, no official has acknowledged that a delegation will be heading to Islamabad, where officials have been on standby for days now for the possible talks

stories.title: U.S. positive on Iran deal but talks still uncertain as ceasefire end nears
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump wants an agreement that would prevent further oil price rises and stock markets shocks but ​has insisted Iran cannot have the means to develop a nuclear weapon

stories.title: Iran executes man over burning of mosque during January protests: report
stories.description: Iran was rocked by ​nationwide anti-government protests earlier this year, ⁠which were repressed ⁠in the biggest crackdown in the ‌Islamic Republic’s history

stories.title: How a U.S.-Iran deal over Hormuz unravelled
Iran-Israel war updates: Deep historical mistrust in Iran toward U.S. government remains, says Iran President
stories.description: Vance has not yet left for Iran talks in Pakistan, says source; Iran says 'no decision' yet on joining new round of U.S. peace talks
